title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.title: Tandoori Chicken Recipe: Charcoal Grilled & Authentic

seo.h1: Authentic Tandoori Chicken Recipe: Charcoal Grilled Perfection
h2Headings: Ingredients for Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken
Step-by-Step: How to Make Tandoori Chicken on a Charcoal Grill

Serving Suggestions for Your Tandoori Chicken
seo.imageAlt: Platter of vibrant red charcoal grilled Tandoori Chicken pieces garnished with cilantro and lemon wedges, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the smoky, authentic flavors of this Tandoori Chicken Recipe, perfectly cooked on a charcoal grill at home.

description: Experience the rich, smoky flavors of authentic Tandoori Chicken, perfectly grilled over charcoal for that irresistible charred finish. This classic Indian dish features succulent chicken marinated in a vibrant blend of yogurt and spices, promising a truly delectable meal. Achieve restaurant-quality taste right in your backyard with this easy-to-follow recipe.

ingredients.notes: skinless, bone-in, cut into 8-10 pieces, deep slits made
for first marinade, or regular chili powder
for second marinade, for color and mild heat
freshly roasted and ground if possible
or any neutral oil like vegetable oil

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make deep slits on the chicken pieces, especially on the thighs and breasts, to allow the marinade to penetrate well. This is crucial for flavor.

instructions.title: First Marinade Application
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 2 tablespoons of lemon juice, 1 teaspoon of salt, and 1 tablespoon of Kashmiri red chili powder. Rub this mixture all over the chicken, ensuring it gets into the slits. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes while you prepare the second marinade. This initial marinade tenderizes and adds a foundational layer of flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, combine the thick yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, the remaining 1.5 tablespoons of Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, and tandoori masala. Add the mustard oil and the optional red food color. Mix everything thoroughly until you have a smooth, vibrant paste. Taste and adjust salt if necessary.

instructions.title: Second Marinade & Resting
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces to the yogurt-spice mixture. Using your hands, generously coat each piece of chicken with the marinade, making sure it goes deep into the slits. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or ideally overnight (12-24 hours) for the best flavor and tenderness. The longer it marinates, the better the result.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30-40 minutes before cooking, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the coals for two-zone cooking: one side with direct high heat and the other with indirect medium heat. The coals should be glowing red with a light ash coating. Clean and oil your grill grates to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the high heat zone of the grill. Sear each side for 2-3 minutes until lightly charred. Then, move the chicken to the indirect heat zone. Close the lid and cook for 25-30 minutes, turning every 5-7 minutes, until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) and the juices run clear. Baste with any leftover marinade or a little oil during the last few turns.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, keeping the chicken moist. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve immediately with lemon wedges, mint chutney, or sliced onions.

tips: For truly authentic Tandoori flavor, ensure your yogurt is very thick. If using regular plain yogurt, strain it through a cheesecloth for at least an hour to remove excess whey.

Do not skip the deep slits in the chicken. They are essential for the marinade to penetrate deeply, ensuring maximum flavor and even cooking. Also, patting the chicken dry prevents the marinade from becoming watery.

Serve your Tandoori Chicken immediately with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice, which brightens the flavors. It pairs wonderfully with a cooling mint chutney, fresh onion rings, or a simple cucumber raita.
article: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is a beloved Indian Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
